steps.title: Identify the constraint-and-solution relationship
steps.content: The previous sentence establishes a limitation (humans cannot perceive four dimensions), and the blank must introduce how Metzinger transcends this through technique. The transition bridges constraint to creative solution.

steps.title: Apply the relationship to the choices
steps.content:
Sentence 1 concedes a limitation (the painting doesn't literally show four dimensions, since we can only see three); sentence 2 shifts to what the painting does achieve (a fascinating glimpse via simultaneous perspectives). The relationship is concession-then-pivot.
  • Choice A — "For example,": signals a specific instance; but sentence 2 isn't an example of the prior limitation. ✗
  • Choice B — "In other words,": signals restatement; but sentence 2 pivots away from the limitation, not restates it. ✗
  • Choice C — "Moreover,": signals adding equal-weight information; but sentence 2 contrasts with the concession, not extends it. ✗
  • Choice D — "That said,": signals "despite what was just conceded" — pivots from the limitation to what the painting achieves. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
